title: Warhammer Quest: Silver Tower - Part Three - It's Lit
body:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=TV3fWx4rQh0

Daniel (@dungeondive) covers the supplementary material for Silver Tower: two White Dwarf issues and the Labyrinth of the Lost novella by Andy Clark. The first White Dwarf is devoted entirely to Silver Tower—lore paragraphs for each hero and adversary, painted diorama spreads, a paint guide, Age of Sigmar warscrolls, and a detailed walkthrough of the first quest that showcases the game's psychedelic table presence.

The second issue features the real highlight: in-depth interviews with the creative team. Sculptors Ben Jefferson, Mark Harrison, David Wiz, and Colin Grayson discuss the models, while rules designer James Hewitt and co-designer Tim Malloy reveal that the sculpts were created first and the game designed around them—the Death Runner's split ability, for instance, exists because two copies appeared on one sprue. The artist Kevin Chin's splash page earns special praise. The Labyrinth of the Lost novella is essentially Andy Clark's idealised playthrough—a short, cheesy, predictable 130-page adventure that Daniel recommends as a fun dungeon-crawl read, championing the novella as a lost art form in genre fiction.

----

Does knowing that the miniatures came before the game mechanics change how you feel about Silver Tower's design?
